title: CSV Cheat Sheet
description: Quick reference for handling CSV files in various environments.

commands.command: wc -l file.csv
commands.description: Count the number of lines in a CSV file
commands.scenario: Count the number of lines in a CSV file

commands.command: head -n 10 file.csv
commands.description: Display the first 10 lines of a CSV file
commands.scenario: Display the first 10 lines of a CSV file

commands.command: tail -n 10 file.csv
commands.description: Display the last 10 lines of a CSV file
commands.scenario: Display the last 10 lines of a CSV file

commands.command:
import csv
with open('file.csv', mode='r') as file:
    reader = csv.DictReader(file)
    data = [row for row in reader]
commands.description: Read a CSV file into a list of dictionaries
commands.scenario: Read a CSV file into a list of dictionaries

commands.command:
import csv
with open('file.csv', mode='w', newline='') as file:
    writer = csv.DictWriter(file, fieldnames=['field1', 'field2'])
    writer.writeheader()
    writer.writerows(data)
commands.description: Write a list of dictionaries to a CSV file
commands.scenario: Write a list of dictionaries to a CSV file

commands.command:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv('file.csv')
commands.description: Read a CSV file into a Pandas DataFrame
commands.scenario: Read a CSV file into a Pandas DataFrame

commands.description: Parse CSV string into an array of objects using PapaParse
commands.scenario: Parse CSV string into an array of objects using PapaParse

commands.command:
const { parse } = require('json2csv');
const csv = parse(data);
commands.description: Convert an array of objects to CSV string using json2csv
commands.scenario: Convert an array of objects to CSV string using json2csv

commands.description: Fetch and parse a CSV file from a URL
commands.scenario: Fetch and parse a CSV file from a URL

commands.command:
LOAD DATA INFILE 'file.csv'
INTO TABLE tablename
FIELDS TERMINATED BY ','
ENCLOSED BY '"'
LINES TERMINATED BY '\n'
IGNORE 1 ROWS;
commands.description: Import CSV file into a SQL table
commands.scenario: Import CSV file into a SQL table

commands.command:
SELECT * FROM tablename
INTO OUTFILE '/path/to/file.csv'
FIELDS TERMINATED BY ','
ENCLOSED BY '"'
LINES TERMINATED BY '\n';
commands.description: Export SQL query results to a CSV file
commands.scenario: Export SQL query results to a CSV file

commands.command:
.mode csv
.import file.csv tablename
commands.description: Use SQLite to import CSV data
commands.scenario: Use SQLite to import CSV data

commands.command: Double-click the CSV file or use File > Open in Excel.
commands.description: Open a CSV file in Excel
commands.scenario: Open a CSV file in Excel

commands.command: File > Save As > Choose CSV (Comma delimited) (*.csv)
commands.description: Save an Excel sheet as a CSV file
commands.scenario: Save an Excel sheet as a CSV file

commands.command: Open Excel > Data > Get External Data > From Text > Choose CSV > Set File Origin to UTF-8
commands.description: Handle CSV import with UTF-8 encoding in Excel
commands.scenario: Handle CSV import with UTF-8 encoding in Excel

commands.command: csvkit - c2d file.csv | csvlook
commands.description: Use csvkit to convert CSV to JSON and pipe it into csvlook for a beautifully formatted, human-readable ASCII table in the terminal.

commands.command: datamash --header-in --input-delim ',' summarize cols 1,2 sum
commands.description: Use datamash to perform high-performance statistical operations (like sum, mean, or min/max) directly on CSV columns via CLI.

commands.command: df = pd.read_csv('file.csv', chunksize=1000)
commands.description: Use the chunksize parameter in Pandas to process massive CSV files that exceed available RAM by iterating through the file in smaller segments.

commands.command: awk -F',' '{print $1, $3}' file.csv
commands.description: Use AWK to quickly extract specific columns (e.g., 1st and 3rd) without loading the entire file into a heavy data processing engine.

commands.command: csvclean file.csv
commands.description: Run csvkit's csvclean utility to identify and fix common CSV errors like unclosed quotes or inconsistent column counts.
